requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) 13Settlement of Export transactions in currencies not having a direct exchange rate
To further liberalise the procedure and facilitate settlement of export transactions where the
invoicing is in a freely convertible currency and the settlement takes place in the currency of
the beneficiary, which though convertible, does not have a direct exchange rate, it has been
decided that AD Category-I banks may permit settlement of such export transactions
(excluding those put through the ACU mechanism), subject to conditions as under:
a) Exporter shall be a customer of the AD Bank,
b) Signed contract / invoice is in a freely convertible currency,
c) The beneficiary is willing to receive the payment in the currency of beneficiary instead of
the original (freely convertible) currency of the invoice/ contract, Letter of Credit as full and
final settlement,
d) AD bank is satisfied with the bonafides of the transactions, and
e) The counterparty to the exporter/ importer of the AD bank is not from a country or
jurisdiction in the updated FATF Public Statement on High Risk & Non Co-operative
Jurisdictions on which FATF has called for counter measures.
requirements.object: the AD Bank, b) Signed contract / invoice is in a freely convertible currency, c) The beneficiary is willing to receive the payment i

A.8 Exports to neighboring countries by road, rail or river
The following procedure should be adopted by exporters for filing original copies of EDF
where exports are made to neighboring countries by road, rail or river transport:
requirements.object: for filing original copies of EDF where exports are made to neighboring countries by road, rail or river transport:

requirements.full_sentence:

exporter or his agent at the Customs station at the border through which the vessel or vehicle
has to pass before crossing over to the foreign territory.

requirements.full_sentence:
A.10 Counter -Trade arrangements with Romania
The Reserve Bank will consider counter trade proposals from Indian exporters with Romania
involving adjustment of value of exports from India against value of imports made into India in
terms of a voluntarily entered arrangement between the concerned parties, subject to the
condition, among others that the Indian exporter should utilize the funds for import of goods
from Romania into India within six months from the date of credit to Escrow Accounts allowed
to be opened.
requirements.object: import of goods from Romania into India within six months from the date of credit to Escrow Accounts allowed to be opened

requirements.object: from the correspondent bank abroad

requirements.full_sentence: (vii) KYC and due diligence on the exporter shall be ensured by the Export Factor.
requirements.object: Export Factor

requirements.full_sentence:
Indian
exporters are required to obtain the approval of the AD Category - I banks/ Exim Bank at
post-award stage before undertaking execution of such contracts.
requirements.object: of the AD Category - I banks/ Exim Bank at post-award stage before undertaking execution of such contracts

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Accordingly, AD banks / Exim Bank may consider awarding post-award approvals without
any monetary limit and permit subsequent changes in the terms of post award approval within
the relevant FEMA guidelines / regulations.
requirements.object: without any monetary limit and permit subsequent changes in the terms of post award approval within the relevan

requirements.full_sentence:
Project and service exporters may approach AD
banks / Exim Bank based on their commercial judgment.
requirements.object: Exim Bank based on their commercial judgment

requirements.full_sentence:
The respective AD bank / Exim Bank
should monitor the projects for which post-award approval has been granted by them.
requirements.object: which post-award approval has been granted by them

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, exporters may use the machinery / equipment for performing any other contract
secured by them in any country subject to the satisfaction of the sponsoring AD Category - I
bank(s) / Exim Bank and also subject to the reporting requirement and would be monitored by
the AD Category - I bank(s) / Exim Bank.
requirements.object: equipment for performing any other contract secured by them in any country subject to the satisfaction of the sponsoring AD Catego

requirements.full_sentence:
b) Inter-Project transfer of funds - AD Category - I bank(s) / Exim Bank may permit exporters
to open, maintain and operate one or more foreign currency account/s in a
currency/currencies of their choice with inter-project transferability of funds in any currency or
country.
requirements.object: maintain and operate one or more foreign currency account/s in a currency/currencies of their choice with inter-project tran

requirements.full_sentence:
c) Deployment of temporary cash surpluses - Subject to monitoring by the AD Category - I
bank(s) / Exim Bank, Project / Service exporters may deploy their temporary cash surpluses,
generated outside India investments in short-term paper abroad including treasury bills and
other monetary instruments with a maturity or remaining maturity of one year or less and the
rating of which should be at least A-1/AAA by Standard & Poor or P-1/-AAA by Moody's or
F1/AAA by Fitch IBCA etc., and as deposits with branches / subsidiaries outside India of AD
Category - I banks in India.
requirements.object: surpluses, generated outside India investments in short-term paper abroad including treasury bills and other monetary inst

requirements.full_sentence:
They should, however, repatriate the
profits of on-site contracts after completion of the contracts.
requirements.object: profits of on-site contracts after completion of the contracts

requirements.full_sentence:
Exporters should apply for necessary permission,
through an AD Category - I banks, to the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank,
giving full particulars of the goods to be exported.
requirements.object: through an AD Category - I banks, to the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank, giving full particulars of the

requirements.full_sentence:
A.16 Export on elongated credit terms
Exporters intending to export goods on elongated credit terms may submit their proposals
giving full particulars through their banks for consideration to the Regional Office concerned of
the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: full particulars through their banks for consideration to the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank
than to Nepal and Bhutan) currency notes of Government of India and Reserve Bank of India up to an amount not exceeding Rs

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Any person resident outside India, not being a citizen of Pakistan and Bangladesh and
also not a traveler coming from and going to Pakistan or Bangladesh, and visiting India may
take outside India currency notes of Government of India and Reserve Bank of India notes up
to an amount not exceeding Rs.
requirements.object: notes of Government of India and Reserve Bank of India notes up to an amount not exceeding Rs

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Customs shall certify the value declared and give running serial number on the two copies
of Export Declaration Form (EDF), submitted by exporter at Non- Electronic Data Interchange
(EDI) port.
requirements.object: and give running serial number on the two copies of Export Declaration Form (EDF), submitted by exporter at Non- Electronic

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Customs shall retain the original EDF for transmission to the Reserve Bank and return the
duplicate copy to the exporter.
requirements.object: for transmission to the Reserve Bank and return the duplicate copy to the exporter

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) At the time of shipment of goods, exporters shall submit the duplicate copy of the EDF to
Customs.
requirements.object: of the EDF to Customs

requirements.full_sentence:
After examining the goods, Customs shall certify the quantity in the form and return
it to the exporter for submission to AD for negotiation or collection of export bills.
requirements.object: the form and return it to the exporter for submission to AD for negotiation or collection of export bills

requirements.timeline: Within 21 days from the date of export
requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Within 21 days from the date of export, exporter shall lodge the duplicate copy together
with relative shipping documents and an extra copy of the invoice to the AD named in the
EDF.
requirements.object: together with relative shipping documents and an extra copy of the invoice to the AD named in the EDF

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) After the documents have been negotiated / sent for collection, the AD shall report the
transaction through Export Data Processing and Monitoring System (EDPMS) to the Reserve
Bank and retain the documents at their end.
requirements.object: Export Data Processing and Monitoring System (EDPMS) to the Reserve Bank and retain the documents at their end

requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) In case of exports made under deferred credit arrangement or to joint ventures abroad
against equity participation or under rupee credit agreement, the number and date of the
Reserve Bank approval and/or number and date of the relative RBI circular shall be recorded
at the appropriate place on the EDF.
requirements.object: appropriate place on the EDF

conditions: Where duplicate copy of EDF is misplaced or lost
requirements.full_sentence:
(vii) Where duplicate copy of EDF is misplaced or lost, AD may accept copy of duplicate EDF
duly certified by Customs.
requirements.object: EDF duly certified by Customs

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The shipping bill shall be submitted in duplicate to the authority concerned (Commissioner
of Customs or the SEZ, if the export is made through it).
requirements.object: to the authority concerned (Commissioner of Customs or the SEZ

requirements.timeline: within 21 days from the date of export for collection
requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) After verifying and authenticating, the authority concerned shall hand over to the exporter,
one copy of the shipping bill marked 'Exchange Control (EC) Copy' for being submitted to the
AD bank within 21 days from the date of export for collection/negotiation of shipping
documents.
requirements.object: exporter, one copy of the shipping bill marked 'Exchange Control (EC) Copy' for being submitted to the AD bank within 21 days from th

requirements.full_sentence: (iii) The manner of disposal of EC copy of Shipping Bill shall be the same as that for EDF.
requirements.object: that for EDF

requirements.full_sentence:
shall be retained by ADs and
may not be submitted to the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: and may not be submitted to the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
B.3 Export of goods through Post
Postal Authorities shall allow export of goods by post only if the original copy of the EDF has
been countersigned by an AD.
requirements.object: by post only if the original copy of the EDF has been countersigned by an AD

requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, EDF which involve sending goods by post should
be first presented by the exporter to an AD for countersignature.
requirements.object: the exporter to an AD for countersignature

requirements.full_sentence: Note: AD Category - I banks may also be guided by the Master Circular on Guarantees and Co-acceptances issued by Department of Banking Regulation.
requirements.object: the Master Circular on Guarantees and Co-acceptances issued by Department of Banking Regulation
